# Render-path benchmark — 512x512: # - gpu_glsl holds ~4.4-5.4 ms across ALL scenes regardless of SDF complexity
#   (sphere vs gyroid) -> the compute path is bound by parallel throughput
#   per-sample SDF cost. Even an iGPU saturates enough to hide the heavier field.
# - gpu_rtx is ~10-13 ms and does NOT beat gpu_glsl here. The NUC iGPU has no

real hardware: so the RT pipeline runs a software BVH fallback. On the

# Render-path benchmark — 512x512: #   discrete RTX 2080 (real RT cores) this flips
#   scenes where the hardware BVH broad-phase pays off (e.g. the thousands of
#   identical crystals in the Geant4/HEP use case).
# - cpu_ir scales with scene complexity (9.8 -> 23.2 ms): serial-per-core
# gpu_glsl speedup over cpu_ir grows with complexity: 1.8x (sphere) -> 5.1x (gyroid).
